verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 is canonically ER-resident but exhibits documented surface translocation under ER stress (drugs, heat shock, oxidative stress), oncogenic transformation in cancer cells, and infection. The protein lacks a classical TM domain but inserts into lipid bilayers with high affinity for negatively charged phospholipids, mediating membrane anchoring and surface-dependent signaling. This qualifies as cell-state-induced ecto-display.
